page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-4 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 4 vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM, yielding a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. Powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor with an x86_64 architecture, the instance allocates 2 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The CPU features 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 (AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, AVX512_BF16) and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU testing placing in the top 25% and Geekbench text processing scoring highly. However, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are relatively constrained, with stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and uncached memory bandwidth tests falling into the bottom 25%. Passmark memory scores are in the bottom 10%, reflecting the low memory density of 2.0 GB per core. Static web serving throughput and single-threaded compression tasks perform at average levels.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation and compute-focused ratio, the c3d-highcpu-4 offers a cost-efficient entry point for CPU-bound tasks that do not require massive memory footprints. The lack of local storage and lower multi-core scaling make it less suitable for heavy database operations or large-scale multi-threaded compilation. It is best suited for lightweight single-threaded processing, basic web serving, text processing, and small-scale model inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-4 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 4 vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it provides 2 physical cores with hyper-threading and a dedicated CPU allocation. The hardware includes AVX-512 and cryptographic instruction support, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core capabilities, particularly in text processing and stress-ng single-core tests, while multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth remain in the lower percentiles. This resource profile makes the instance highly cost-efficient for single-threaded workloads, basic web serving, and lightweight text processing, though it is less suited for memory-intensive databases or heavy multi-threaded compilation.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-4 is a compute-optimized instance with 4 vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 9B14 CPU, offering strong single-core performance for text processing and lightweight web serving.
